mechanism_narrative: Persistent growth in real GDP (GDPC1) signals strong economic demand, which increases corporate earnings visibility and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods of GDPC1 growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in CPIAUCSL below 190 indicates disinflationary pressure, prompting central bank rate cuts that lower discount rates and generate positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in disinflationary periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in FEDFUNDS and increases in INDPRO signal accommodative monetary policy supporting industrial activity, creating positive S&P 500 momentum over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in co-occurring FEDFUNDS/INDPRO signals are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or interaction effect size < 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained UNRATE above 5.5% indicates labor market slack, which suppresses wage growth and corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in high UNRATE regimes are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC > -0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly gains in PAYEMS above 150k signal robust job creation, boosting consumer spending and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in high PAYEMS periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in HOUST above 2100 units indicates housing demand strength, which correlates with positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through sectoral spillovers.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in high HOUST regimes are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in RSAFS and rising CPIAUCSL indicate weak consumer spending and inflationary pressures, creating negative S&P 500 momentum over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in co-occurring RSAFS/CPIAUCSL signals are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or interaction effect size < -0.15.

mechanism_narrative: A FEDFUNDS rate above 4.5% during a 3-month period signals monetary tightening, which increases borrowing costs and generates negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in high FEDFUNDS regimes are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC > -0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
